```{r}
#========================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
#========================================

summary_zipo1 <- data.frame(
  Mean_Coverage = mean(results_zipo1$Coverage),
  Mean_Width    = mean(results_zipo1$Width),
  Mean_ME       = mean(results_zipo1$ME),
  Mean_RMSE     = mean(results_zipo1$RMSE),
  Mean_MAE      = mean(results_zipo1$MAE),
  Mean_MAPE     = mean(results_zipo1$MAPE)
)



summary_zipo1

```
#80
```{r}


#========================================
# STORAGE
#========================================

results_zipo2 <- data.frame()

#========================================
# 100 REPLICATIONS
#========================================

set.seed(123)

for(i in 1:100){

  cat("\n====================================\n")
  cat("ZIPO2 : REPLICATION", i, "\n")
  cat("====================================\n")

  #====================================
  # CREATE 80:20 SPLIT
  #====================================

  train_id2 <- sample(
    1:nrow(data1),
    0.80 * nrow(data1)
  )

  train_2 <- data1[train_id2, ]
  test_2  <- data1[-train_id2, ]

  train_2 <- na.omit(train_2)
  test_2  <- na.omit(test_2)

  #====================================
  # FIT MODEL
  #====================================

  ZIPO_2 <- tryCatch(

    vglm(
      visits ~ health +
        insurance +
        income +
        age,

      zipoissonff,
      data = train_2
    ),

    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if(is.null(ZIPO_2)) next

  #====================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #====================================

  pred_zipo2 <- predict(
    ZIPO_2,
    newdata = test_2,
    type = "response"
  )

  #====================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #====================================

  train_pred_zipo2 <- predict(
    ZIPO_2,
    newdata = train_2,
    type = "response"
  )

  #====================================
  # RESIDUALS
  #====================================

  resid_zipo2 <- train_2$visits -
    train_pred_zipo2

  B <- 1000

  boot_zipo2 <- matrix(
    NA,
    nrow = nrow(test_2),
    ncol = B
  )

  #====================================
  # RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
  #====================================

  for(b in 1:B){

    sampled_resid2 <- sample(
      resid_zipo2,
      nrow(test_2),
      replace = TRUE
    )

    boot_zipo2[, b] <-
      pred_zipo2 + sampled_resid2
  }

  boot_zipo2[boot_zipo2 < 0] <- 0
  boot_zipo2 <- round(boot_zipo2)

  #====================================
  # INTERVALS
  #====================================

  lower_zipo2 <- apply(
    boot_zipo2,
    1,
    quantile,
    probs = 0.025
  )

  upper_zipo2 <- apply(
    boot_zipo2,
    1,
    quantile,
    probs = 0.975
  )

  #====================================
  # COVERAGE & WIDTH
  #====================================

  coverage_zipo2 <- mean(
    test_2$visits >= lower_zipo2 &
      test_2$visits <= upper_zipo2
  )

  width_zipo2 <- mean(
    upper_zipo2 - lower_zipo2
  )

  #====================================
  # ACCURACY
  #====================================

  acc_zipo2 <- accuracy(
    pred_zipo2,
    test_2$visits
  )

  #====================================
  # STORE RESULTS
  #====================================

  results_zipo2 <- rbind(
    results_zipo2,
    data.frame(
      Replication = i,
      Coverage = coverage_zipo2,
      Width = width_zipo2,
      ME = acc_zipo2["Test set","ME"],
      RMSE = acc_zipo2["Test set","RMSE"],
      MAE = acc_zipo2["Test set","MAE"],
      MAPE = acc_zipo2["Test set","MAPE"]
    )
  )
}

summary_zipo2 <- data.frame(
  Mean_Coverage = mean(results_zipo2$Coverage),
  Mean_Width    = mean(results_zipo2$Width),
  Mean_ME       = mean(results_zipo2$ME),
  Mean_RMSE     = mean(results_zipo2$RMSE),
  Mean_MAE      = mean(results_zipo2$MAE),
  Mean_MAPE     = mean(results_zipo2$MAPE)
)

summary_zipo2

```

#90
```{r}

#========================================
# STORAGE
#========================================

results_zipo3 <- data.frame()

#========================================
# 100 REPLICATIONS
#========================================

set.seed(123)

for(i in 1:100){

  cat("\n====================================\n")
  cat("ZIPO3 : REPLICATION", i, "\n")
  cat("====================================\n")

  #====================================
  # CREATE 90:10 SPLIT
  #====================================

  train_id3 <- sample(
    1:nrow(data1),
    0.90 * nrow(data1)
  )

  train_3 <- data1[train_id3, ]
  test_3  <- data1[-train_id3, ]

  train_3 <- na.omit(train_3)
  test_3  <- na.omit(test_3)

  #====================================
  # FIT MODEL
  #====================================

  ZIPO_3 <- tryCatch(

    vglm(
      visits ~ health +
        insurance +
        income +
        age,

      zipoissonff,
      data = train_3
    ),

    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if(is.null(ZIPO_3)) next

  #====================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #====================================

  pred_zipo3 <- predict(
    ZIPO_3,
    newdata = test_3,
    type = "response"
  )

  #====================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #====================================

  train_pred_zipo3 <- predict(
    ZIPO_3,
    newdata = train_3,
    type = "response"
  )

  #====================================
  # RESIDUALS
  #====================================

  resid_zipo3 <- train_3$visits -
    train_pred_zipo3

  B <- 1000

  boot_zipo3 <- matrix(
    NA,
    nrow = nrow(test_3),
    ncol = B
  )

  #====================================
  # RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
  #====================================

  for(b in 1:B){

    sampled_resid3 <- sample(
      resid_zipo3,
      nrow(test_3),
      replace = TRUE
    )

    boot_zipo3[, b] <-
      pred_zipo3 + sampled_resid3
  }

  boot_zipo3[boot_zipo3 < 0] <- 0
  boot_zipo3 <- round(boot_zipo3)

  #====================================
  # INTERVALS
  #====================================

  lower_zipo3 <- apply(
    boot_zipo3,
    1,
    quantile,
    probs = 0.025
  )

  upper_zipo3 <- apply(
    boot_zipo3,
    1,
    quantile,
    probs = 0.975
  )

  #====================================
  # COVERAGE & WIDTH
  #====================================

  coverage_zipo3 <- mean(
    test_3$visits >= lower_zipo3 &
      test_3$visits <= upper_zipo3
  )

  width_zipo3 <- mean(
    upper_zipo3 - lower_zipo3
  )

  #====================================
  # ACCURACY
  #====================================

  acc_zipo3 <- forecast::accuracy(
    pred_zipo3,
    test_3$visits
  )

  #====================================
  # STORE RESULTS
  #====================================

  results_zipo3 <- rbind(
    results_zipo3,
    data.frame(
      Replication = i,
      Coverage = coverage_zipo3,
      Width = width_zipo3,
      ME = acc_zipo3["Test set","ME"],
      RMSE = acc_zipo3["Test set","RMSE"],
      MAE = acc_zipo3["Test set","MAE"],
      MAPE = acc_zipo3["Test set","MAPE"]
    )
  )
}

summary_zipo3 <- data.frame(
  Mean_Coverage = mean(results_zipo3$Coverage),
  Mean_Width    = mean(results_zipo3$Width),
  Mean_ME       = mean(results_zipo3$ME),
  Mean_RMSE     = mean(results_zipo3$RMSE),
  Mean_MAE      = mean(results_zipo3$MAE),
  Mean_MAPE     = mean(results_zipo3$MAPE)
)

summary_zipo3
  
```


##CPZI
```{r}
   
library(glmmTMB)
CPZI_1= glmmTMB(
  visits ~ health+insurance+income+age,
  ziformula = ~ income + insurance,
  family = compois(),
  data = train_1)

CPZI_2= glmmTMB(
  visits ~ health+insurance+income+age,
  ziformula = ~ income + insurance,
  family =compois() ,
  data = train_2)

CPZI_3= glmmTMB(
  visits ~ health+insurance+income+age,
  ziformula = ~ income + insurance,
  family = compois(),
  data = train_3)

```

##prediction
```{r}
# COM-Poisson ZI
CPZI_PR1 = predict(CPZI_1, newdata = test_1, type = "response")
CPZI_PR2 = predict(CPZI_2, newdata = test_2, type = "response")
CPZI_PR3 = predict(CPZI_3, newdata = test_3, type = "response")
```

```{r}
train_pred_comp1 <- predict(CPZI_1,newdata = train_1,type = "response")
resid_comp1 <- train_1$visits -train_pred_comp1


train_pred_comp2 <- predict(CPZI_2,newdata = train_2,type = "response")
resid_comp2 <- train_2$visits -train_pred_comp2


train_pred_comp3 <- predict(CPZI_3,newdata = train_3,type = "response")
resid_comp3 <- train_3$visits -train_pred_comp3
```
#error
```{r}
# 70:30
acc_cpzi1 <- forecast::accuracy(CPZI_PR1, test_1$visits)

# 80:20
acc_cpzi2 <- forecast::accuracy(CPZI_PR2, test_2$visits)

# 90:10
acc_cpzi3 <- forecast::accuracy(CPZI_PR3, test_3$visits)


CPZI_Accuracy <- data.frame(
  Split = c("70:30", "80:20", "90:10"),
  MAE = c(
    acc_cpzi1["Test set", "MAE"],
    acc_cpzi2["Test set", "MAE"],
    acc_cpzi3["Test set", "MAE"]
  ),
  RMSE = c(
    acc_cpzi1["Test set", "RMSE"],
    acc_cpzi2["Test set", "RMSE"],
    acc_cpzi3["Test set", "RMSE"]
  ),
  ME = c(
    acc_cpzi1["Test set", "ME"],
    acc_cpzi2["Test set", "ME"],
    acc_cpzi3["Test set", "ME"]
  )
)

CPZI_Accuracy

CPZI_Final <- data.frame(

  Split = CPZI_Accuracy$Split,

  RMSE = CPZI_Accuracy$RMSE,

  MAE = CPZI_Accuracy$MAE,

  ME = CPZI_Accuracy$ME,

  Coverage = c(
    coverage_cpzi1,
    coverage_cpzi2,
    coverage_cpzi3
  ),

  Width = c(
    width_cpzi1,
    width_cpzi2,
    width_cpzi3
  ),

  Convergence = c(
    100,
    100,
    100
  )
)

CPZI_Final

```


##pi-residual
```{r}
B <- 500

boot_cpzi1 <- matrix(
  NA,
  nrow = nrow(test_1),
  ncol = B
)

for(b in 1:B){

  res_boot <- sample(
    resid_comp1,
    nrow(test_1),
    replace = TRUE
  )

  boot_cpzi1[, b] <- (
    CPZI_PR1 +
    res_boot
  )
}
#=====================================
# NONNEGATIVE COUNTS
#=====================================

boot_cpzi1[boot_cpzi1 < 0] <- 0

# Round bootstrap predictions
boot_cpzi1 <- round(
  boot_cpzi1
)

#=====================================
# PREDICTION INTERVAL
#=====================================

lower_cpzi1 <- apply(
  boot_cpzi1,
  1,
  quantile,
  probs = 0.025
)

upper_cpzi1 <- apply(
  boot_cpzi1,
  1,
  quantile,
  probs = 0.975
)

#=====================================
# COVERAGE
#=====================================

coverage_cpzi1 <- mean(
  test_1$visits >= lower_cpzi1 &
  test_1$visits <= upper_cpzi1
)

coverage_cpzi1

#=====================================
# WIDTH
#=====================================

width_cpzi1 <- mean(
  upper_cpzi1 - lower_cpzi1
)

width_cpzi1
```

```{r}
B <- 500

boot_cpzi2 <- matrix(
  NA,
  nrow = nrow(test_2),
  ncol = B
)

for(b in 1:B){

  res_boot <- sample(
    resid_comp2,
    nrow(test_2),
    replace = TRUE
  )

  boot_cpzi2[, b] <- (
    CPZI_PR2 +
    res_boot
  )
}

#=====================================
# NONNEGATIVE COUNTS
#=====================================

boot_cpzi2[boot_cpzi2 < 0] <- 0

# Round bootstrap predictions
boot_cpzi2 <- round(
  boot_cpzi2
)

#=====================================
# PREDICTION INTERVAL
#=====================================

lower_cpzi2 <- apply(
  boot_cpzi2,
  1,
  quantile,
  probs = 0.025
)

upper_cpzi2 <- apply(
  boot_cpzi2,
  1,
  quantile,
  probs = 0.975
)

#=====================================
# COVERAGE
#=====================================

coverage_cpzi2 <- mean(
  test_2$visits >= lower_cpzi2 &
  test_2$visits <= upper_cpzi2
)

coverage_cpzi2

#=====================================
# WIDTH
#=====================================

width_cpzi2 <- mean(
  upper_cpzi2 - lower_cpzi2
)

width_cpzi2
```

```{r}
B <- 500

boot_cpzi3 <- matrix(
  NA,
  nrow = nrow(test_3),
  ncol = B
)

for(b in 1:B){

  res_boot <- sample(
    resid_comp3,
    nrow(test_3),
    replace = TRUE
  )

  boot_cpzi3[, b] <- (
    CPZI_PR3 +
    res_boot
  )
}

#=====================================
# NONNEGATIVE COUNTS
#=====================================

boot_cpzi3[boot_cpzi3 < 0] <- 0

# Round bootstrap predictions
boot_cpzi3 <- round(
  boot_cpzi3
)

#=====================================
# PREDICTION INTERVAL
#=====================================

lower_cpzi3 <- apply(
  boot_cpzi3,
  1,
  quantile,
  probs = 0.025
)

upper_cpzi3 <- apply(
  boot_cpzi3,
  1,
  quantile,
  probs = 0.975
)

#=====================================
# COVERAGE
#=====================================

coverage_cpzi3 <- mean(
  test_3$visits >= lower_cpzi3 &
  test_3$visits <= upper_cpzi3
)

coverage_cpzi3

#=====================================
# WIDTH
#=====================================

width_cpzi3 <- mean(
  upper_cpzi3 - lower_cpzi3
)

width_cpzi3  

CPZI_Final <- data.frame(

  Split = CPZI_Accuracy$Split,

  RMSE = CPZI_Accuracy$RMSE,

  MAE = CPZI_Accuracy$MAE,

  ME = CPZI_Accuracy$ME,

  Coverage = c(
    coverage_cpzi1,
    coverage_cpzi2,
    coverage_cpzi3
  ),

  Width = c(
    width_cpzi1,
    width_cpzi2,
    width_cpzi3
  ),

  Convergence = c(
    100,
    100,
    100
  )
)

CPZI_Final

```

##GPZI

```{r}
library(glmmTMB)
library(forecast)

results_gpzi1 <- data.frame()

set.seed(123)

R <- 100
B <- 300

for(i in 1:R){

  cat("Replication:", i, "\n")

  #==================================
  # SPLIT
  #==================================

  train_id <- sample(
    1:nrow(data1),
    size = floor(0.70*nrow(data1))
  )

  train_1 <- data1[train_id, ]
  test_1  <- data1[-train_id, ]

  #==================================
  # FIT GPZI
  #==================================

  fit1 <- tryCatch(

    glmmTMB(
      visits ~ health +
        insurance +
        income +
        age,

      ziformula =
        ~ health +
        insurance,

      family = genpois(),

      data = train_1
    ),

    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if(is.null(fit1)) next

  #==================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #==================================

  pred <- predict(
    fit1,
    newdata = test_1,
    type = "response"
  )

  #==================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #==================================

  train_pred <- predict(
    fit1,
    newdata = train_1,
    type = "response"
  )

  #==================================
  # RESIDUALS
  #==================================

  resid <- train_1$visits -
    train_pred

  #==================================
  # BOOTSTRAP PI
  #==================================

  boot <- matrix(
    NA,
    nrow = nrow(test_1),
    ncol = B
  )

  for(b in 1:B){

    samp_res <- sample(
      resid,
      nrow(test_1),
      replace = TRUE
    )

    boot[,b] <- pred + samp_res
  }

  boot[boot < 0] <- 0

  lower <- apply(
    boot,
    1,
    quantile,
    probs = 0.025
  )

  upper <- apply(
    boot,
    1,
    quantile,
    probs = 0.975
  )

  #==================================
  # COVERAGE
  #==================================

  coverage_gpzi1 <- mean(
    test_1$visits >= lower &
      test_1$visits <= upper
  )

  width_gpzi1 <- mean(
    upper - lower
  )

  #==================================
  # ACCURACY
  #==================================

 rmse_gpzi1 <- sqrt(
  mean(
    (actual1 - pred)^2
  )
)

mae_gpzi1 <- mean(
  abs(actual1 - pred)
)

me_gpzi1 <- mean(
  actual1 - pred
)

mpe_gpzi1 <- mean(
  (actual1 - pred) /
    pmax(actual1,1)
) * 100

mape_gpzi1 <- mean(
  abs(
    (actual1 - pred) /
      pmax(actual1,1)
  )
) * 100

}
  results_gpzi1 <- rbind(
  results_gpzi1,
  data.frame(
    RMSE = rmse_gpzi1,
    MAE = mae_gpzi1,
    ME = me_gpzi1,
    MPE = mpe_gpzi1,
    MAPE = mape_gpzi1,
    Coverage = coverage_gpzi1,
    Width = width_gpzi1
  )
)
   results_gpzi1 
```


#80
```{r}
#========================================================
# FAST + STABLE GPZI MODEL
# 80:20 SPLIT
# 100 REPLICATIONS
# GENERALIZED POISSON ZERO-INFLATED
#========================================================
results_gpzi2 <- data.frame()

set.seed(123)

R <- 100
B <- 300

for(i in 1:R){

  cat("Replication:", i, "\n")

  train_id <- sample(
    1:nrow(data1),
    size = floor(0.80*nrow(data1))
  )

  train_2 <- data1[train_id, ]
  test_2 <- data1[-train_id, ]

  fit2 <- tryCatch(
    glmmTMB(
      visits ~ health +
        insurance +
        income +
        age,
      ziformula =
        ~ health +
        insurance,
      family = genpois(),
      data = train_2
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if(is.null(fit2)) next

  pred <- predict(
    fit2,
    newdata = test_2,
    type = "response"
  )
#==================================
# TRAIN PREDICTIONS
#==================================

train_pred <- predict(
  fit2,
  newdata = train_2,
  type = "response"
)

#==================================
# RESIDUALS
#==================================

resid <- train_2$visits -
  train_pred

#==================================
# BOOTSTRAP STORAGE
#==================================

boot <- matrix(
  NA,
  nrow = nrow(test_2),
  ncol = B
)

#==================================
# RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
#==================================

for(b in 1:B){

  samp_res <- sample(
    resid,
    nrow(test_2),
    replace = TRUE
  )

  boot[,b] <- pred + samp_res
}

#==================================
# NON-NEGATIVE COUNTS
#==================================

boot[boot < 0] <- 0

#==================================
# PREDICTION INTERVALS
#==================================

lower <- apply(
  boot,
  1,
  quantile,
  probs = 0.025,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

upper <- apply(
  boot,
  1,
  quantile,
  probs = 0.975,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

#==================================
# COVERAGE
#==================================

coverage_gpzi2 <- mean(
  test_2$visits >= lower &
  test_2$visits <= upper,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

#==================================
# WIDTH
#==================================

width_gpzi2 <- mean(
  upper - lower,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
  actual2 <- test_2$visits

  rmse_gpzi2 <- sqrt(
    mean((actual2 - pred)^2,
         na.rm = TRUE)
  )

  mae_gpzi2 <- mean(
    abs(actual2 - pred),
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  me_gpzi2 <- mean(
    actual2 - pred,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  mpe_gpzi2 <- mean(
    (actual2 - pred) /
      pmax(actual2,1),
    na.rm = TRUE
  )*100

  mape_gpzi2 <- mean(
    abs(
      (actual2 - pred) /
        pmax(actual2,1)
    ),
    na.rm = TRUE
  )*100

  results_gpzi2 <- rbind(
    results_gpzi2,
    data.frame(
      RMSE = rmse_gpzi2,
      MAE = mae_gpzi2,
      ME = me_gpzi2,
      MPE = mpe_gpzi2,
      MAPE = mape_gpzi2
    )
  )
}

final_gpzi2 <- data.frame(
  Split = "80:20",
  RMSE = mean(results_gpzi2$RMSE),
  MAE = mean(results_gpzi2$MAE),
  ME = mean(results_gpzi2$ME),
  MPE = mean(results_gpzi2$MPE),
  MAPE = mean(results_gpzi2$MAPE)
)

final_gpzi2
  
```

#90
```{r}


results_gpzi3 <- data.frame()

set.seed(123)

R <- 100
B <- 300

for(i in 1:R){

  cat("\n=================================\n")
  cat("GPZI 90:10 REPLICATION", i, "\n")
  cat("=================================\n")

  #==================================
  # 90:10 SPLIT
  #==================================

  train_id <- sample(
    1:nrow(data1),
    size = floor(
      0.90*nrow(data1)
    )
  )

  train_3 <- data1[train_id, ]

  test_3 <- data1[-train_id, ]

  #==================================
  # FIT GPZI MODEL
  #==================================

  fit3 <- tryCatch(

    glmmTMB(

      visits ~

        health +

        insurance +

        income +

        age,

      ziformula =

        ~ health +

          insurance,

      family = genpois(),

      data = train_3

    ),

    error = function(e){

      cat("MODEL FAILED\n")

      return(NULL)

    }

  )

  if(is.null(fit3)){

    next

  }

  #==================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #==================================

  pred <- predict(

    fit3,

    newdata = test_3,

    type = "response"

  )

  actual3 <- test_3$visits

  #==================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #==================================

  train_pred <- predict(

    fit3,

    newdata = train_3,

    type = "response"

  )

  #==================================
  # RESIDUALS
  #==================================

  resid <- (

    train_3$visits -

      train_pred

  )

  resid <- resid[
    is.finite(resid)
  ]

  #==================================
  # BOOTSTRAP STORAGE
  #==================================

  boot <- matrix(

    NA,

    nrow = length(actual3),

    ncol = B

  )

  #==================================
  # RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
  #==================================

  for(b in 1:B){

    samp_res <- sample(

      resid,

      size = length(actual3),

      replace = TRUE

    )

    boot[,b] <- (

      pred +

        samp_res

    )

  }

  #==================================
  # NONNEGATIVE COUNTS
  #==================================

  boot[
    boot < 0
  ] <- 0

  #==================================
  # PREDICTION INTERVALS
  #==================================

  lower <- apply(

    boot,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.025,

    na.rm = TRUE

  )

  upper <- apply(

    boot,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.975,

    na.rm = TRUE

  )

  #==================================
  # COVERAGE
  #==================================

  coverage_gpzi3 <- mean(

    actual3 >= lower &

      actual3 <= upper,

    na.rm = TRUE

  )

  #==================================
  # WIDTH
  #==================================

  width_gpzi3 <- mean(

    upper - lower,

    na.rm = TRUE

  )

  #==================================
  # ERROR MEASURES
  #==================================

  rmse_gpzi3 <- sqrt(

    mean(

      (actual3 - pred)^2,

      na.rm = TRUE

    )

  )

  mae_gpzi3 <- mean(

    abs(actual3 - pred),

    na.rm = TRUE

  )

  me_gpzi3  <- mean(

    actual3 - pred,

    na.rm = TRUE

  )

  mpe_gpzi3  <- mean(

    (actual3 - pred) /

      pmax(actual3,1),

    na.rm = TRUE

  ) * 100

  mape_gpzi3  <- mean(

    abs(

      (actual3 - pred) /

        pmax(actual3,1)

    ),

    na.rm = TRUE

  ) * 100

  
}


```


#ZIADD
```{r}
#========================================================
# FAST + STABLE ZERO-INFLATED ADDITIVE MODEL
# 70:30 SPLIT
# 100 REPLICATIONS
# GAM + RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP PI
#========================================================

#========================================================
# LOAD PACKAGE
#========================================================

library(mgcv)

#========================================================
# STORAGE OBJECT
#========================================================

results_add1 <- data.frame()

#========================================================
# REPRODUCIBILITY
#========================================================

set.seed(123)

#========================================================
# NUMBER OF REPLICATIONS
#========================================================

R <- 100

#========================================================
# NUMBER OF BOOTSTRAPS
#========================================================

B <- 300

#========================================================
# START LOOP
#========================================================

for(i in 1:R){

  cat("\n====================================\n")

  cat("ZI-ADDITIVE1 : REPLICATION", i, "\n")

  cat("====================================\n")

  #======================================================
  # CREATE TRAIN-TEST SPLIT
  #======================================================

  train_id1 <- sample(

    1:nrow(data1),

    size = floor(
      0.70 * nrow(data1)
    )
  )

  train_1 <- data1[train_id1, ]

  test_1 <- data1[-train_id1, ]

  #======================================================
  # REMOVE MISSING VALUES
  #======================================================

  train_1 <- na.omit(train_1)

  test_1 <- na.omit(test_1)

  #======================================================
  # FIT MODEL
  #======================================================

  zi_add_tr1 <- tryCatch(

    suppressWarnings(

      gam(

        list(

          visits ~

            s(age) +

            s(income) +

            insurance +

            health,

          ~

            s(age) +

            health +

            insurance
        ),

        family = ziplss(),

        data = train_1,

        method = "REML"
      )
    ),

    error = function(e){

      cat(
        "MODEL FAILED\n"
      )

      return(NULL)
    }
  )

  #======================================================
  # SKIP FAILED MODELS
  #======================================================

  if(is.null(zi_add_tr1)){

    next
  }

  #======================================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  hurdle_add_pr1 <- tryCatch(

    predict(

      zi_add_tr1,

      newdata = test_1,

      type = "response"
    ),

    error = function(e){

      rep(

        mean(train_1$visits),

        nrow(test_1)
      )
    }
  )

  #======================================================
  # HANDLE MATRIX PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  if(is.matrix(hurdle_add_pr1)){

    hurdle_add_pr1 <- hurdle_add_pr1[,1]
  }

  #======================================================
  # REMOVE INVALID VALUES
  #======================================================

  keep_test <- complete.cases(

    test_1$visits,

    hurdle_add_pr1

  ) &

    is.finite(hurdle_add_pr1)

  actual_test <- test_1$visits[
    keep_test
  ]

  hurdle_add_pr1 <- hurdle_add_pr1[
    keep_test
  ]

  #======================================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  train_pred_add1 <- predict(

    zi_add_tr1,

    newdata = train_1,

    type = "response"
  )

  #======================================================
  # HANDLE MATRIX PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  if(is.matrix(train_pred_add1)){

    train_pred_add1 <- train_pred_add1[,1]
  }

  #======================================================
  # RESPONSE RESIDUALS
  #======================================================

  resid_add1 <- (

    train_1$visits -

      train_pred_add1
  )

  resid_add1 <- resid_add1[
    is.finite(resid_add1)
  ]

  #======================================================
  # STORAGE MATRIX
  #======================================================

  boot_add1 <- matrix(

    NA,

    nrow = length(actual_test),

    ncol = B
  )

  #======================================================
  # RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
  #======================================================

  for(b in 1:B){

    sampled_resid1 <- sample(

      resid_add1,

      size = length(actual_test),

      replace = TRUE
    )

    #----------------------------------
    # VARIANCE INFLATION
    #----------------------------------

    boot_add1[, b] <- (

      hurdle_add_pr1 +

        1.5 * sampled_resid1
    )
  }

  #======================================================
  # NON-NEGATIVE COUNTS
  #======================================================

  boot_add1[
    boot_add1 < 0
  ] <- 0

  #======================================================
  # PREDICTION INTERVALS
  #======================================================

  lower_add1 <- apply(

    boot_add1,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.025,

    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  upper_add1 <- apply(

    boot_add1,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.975,

    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  #======================================================
  # ROUND AFTER QUANTILES
  #======================================================

  lower_add1 <- pmax(

    0,

    round(lower_add1)
  )

  upper_add1 <- pmax(

    0,

    round(upper_add1)
  )

  #======================================================
  # COVERAGE
  #======================================================

  coverage_add1 <- mean(

    actual_test >= lower_add1 &

      actual_test <= upper_add1
  )
 coverage_add1
  #======================================================
  # WIDTH
  #======================================================

  width_add1 <- mean(

    upper_add1 -

      lower_add1
  )

  #======================================================
  # RMSE
  #======================================================
#======================================
# ERROR MEASURES
#======================================

rmse_add1 <- sqrt(
  mean(
    (actual3  - hurdle_add_pr1)^2,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
)

mae_add1 <- mean(
  abs(
    actual3  - hurdle_add_pr1
  ),
  na.rm = TRUE
)

me_add1 <- mean(
  actual3  - hurdle_add_pr1,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

mpe_add1 <- mean(
  (actual3 - hurdle_add_pr1) /
    pmax(actual3 ,1),
  na.rm = TRUE
) * 100

mape_add1 <- mean(
  abs(
    (actual3 - hurdle_add_pr1) /
      pmax(actual3 ,1)
  ),
  na.rm = TRUE
) * 100}


c(
  RMSE = rmse_add1,
  MAE = mae_add1,
  ME = me_add1,
  MPE = mpe_add1,
  MAPE = mape_add1
) 
```

#80
```{r}
#========================================================
# FAST + STABLE ZERO-INFLATED ADDITIVE MODEL
# 80:20 SPLIT
# 100 REPLICATIONS
# GAM + RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP PI
#========================================================
#========================================================
# STORAGE OBJECT
#========================================================

results_add2 <- data.frame()

#========================================================
# REPRODUCIBILITY
#========================================================

set.seed(123)

#========================================================
# NUMBER OF REPLICATIONS
#========================================================

R <- 100

#========================================================
# NUMBER OF BOOTSTRAPS
#========================================================

B <- 300

#========================================================
# START LOOP
#========================================================

for(i in 1:R){

  cat("\n====================================\n")

  cat("ZI-ADDITIVE2 : REPLICATION", i, "\n")

  cat("====================================\n")

  #======================================================
  # CREATE TRAIN-TEST SPLIT
  #======================================================

  train_id2 <- sample(

    1:nrow(data1),

    size = floor(
      0.80 * nrow(data1)
    )
  )

  train_2 <- data1[train_id2, ]

  test_2 <- data1[-train_id2, ]

  #======================================================
  # REMOVE MISSING VALUES
  #======================================================

  train_2 <- na.omit(train_2)

  test_2 <- na.omit(test_2)

  #======================================================
  # FIT MODEL
  #======================================================

  zi_add_tr2 <- tryCatch(

    suppressWarnings(

      gam(

        list(

          visits ~

            s(age) +

            s(income) +

            insurance +

            health,

          ~

            s(age) +

            health +

            insurance
        ),

        family = ziplss(),

        data = train_2,

        method = "REML"
      )
    ),

    error = function(e){

      cat(
        "MODEL FAILED\n"
      )

      return(NULL)
    }
  )

  #======================================================
  # SKIP FAILED MODELS
  #======================================================

  if(is.null(zi_add_tr2)){

    next
  }

  #======================================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  hurdle_add_pr2 <- tryCatch(

    predict(

      zi_add_tr2,

      newdata = test_2,

      type = "response"
    ),

    error = function(e){

      rep(

        mean(train_2$visits),

        nrow(test_2)
      )
    }
  )

  #======================================================
  # HANDLE MATRIX PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  if(is.matrix(hurdle_add_pr2)){

    hurdle_add_pr2 <- hurdle_add_pr2[,1]
  }

  #======================================================
  # REMOVE INVALID VALUES
  #======================================================

  keep_test <- complete.cases(

    test_2$visits,

    hurdle_add_pr2

  ) &

    is.finite(hurdle_add_pr2)

  actual_test <- test_2$visits[
    keep_test
  ]

  hurdle_add_pr2 <- hurdle_add_pr2[
    keep_test
  ]

  #======================================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  train_pred_add2 <- predict(

    zi_add_tr2,

    newdata = train_2,

    type = "response"
  )

  #======================================================
  # HANDLE MATRIX PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  if(is.matrix(train_pred_add2)){

    train_pred_add2 <- train_pred_add2[,1]
  }

  #======================================================
  # RESPONSE RESIDUALS
  #======================================================

  resid_add2 <- (

    train_2$visits -

      train_pred_add2
  )

  resid_add2 <- resid_add2[
    is.finite(resid_add2)
  ]

  #======================================================
  # STORAGE MATRIX
  #======================================================

  boot_add2 <- matrix(

    NA,

    nrow = length(actual_test),

    ncol = B
  )

  #======================================================
  # RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
  #======================================================

  for(b in 1:B){

    sampled_resid2 <- sample(

      resid_add2,

      size = length(actual_test),

      replace = TRUE
    )

    boot_add2[, b] <- (

      hurdle_add_pr2 +

        1.5 * sampled_resid2
    )
  }

  #======================================================
  # NON-NEGATIVE COUNTS
  #======================================================

  boot_add2[
    boot_add2 < 0
  ] <- 0

  #======================================================
  # PREDICTION INTERVALS
  #======================================================

  lower_add2 <- apply(

    boot_add2,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.025,

    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  upper_add2 <- apply(

    boot_add2,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.975,

    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  #======================================================
  # ROUND AFTER QUANTILES
  #======================================================

  lower_add2 <- pmax(

    0,

    round(lower_add2)
  )

  upper_add2 <- pmax(

    0,

    round(upper_add2)
  )

  #======================================================
  # COVERAGE
  #======================================================

  coverage_add2 <- mean(

    actual_test >= lower_add2 &

      actual_test <= upper_add2
  )

  #======================================================
  # WIDTH
  #======================================================

  width_add2 <- mean(

    upper_add2 -

      lower_add2
  )

  #======================================================
  # RMSE
  #======================================================

  #======================================
# ERROR MEASURES : 80:20 SPLIT
#======================================

rmse_add2 <- sqrt(
  mean(
    (actual2 - hurdle_add_pr2)^2,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
)

mae_add2 <- mean(
  abs(actual2 - hurdle_add_pr2),
  na.rm = TRUE
)

me_add2 <- mean(
  actual2 - hurdle_add_pr2,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

mpe_add2 <- mean(
  (actual2 - hurdle_add_pr2) /
    pmax(actual2, 1),
  na.rm = TRUE
) * 100

mape_add2 <- mean(
  abs(
    (actual2 - hurdle_add_pr2) /
      pmax(actual2, 1)
  ),
  na.rm = TRUE
) * 100}



  
```
#90
```{r}
#========================================================
# FAST + STABLE ZERO-INFLATED ADDITIVE MODEL
# 90:10 SPLIT
# 100 REPLICATIONS
# GAM + RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP PI
#========================================================

results_add3 <- data.frame()

#========================================================
# REPRODUCIBILITY
#========================================================

set.seed(123)

#========================================================
# NUMBER OF REPLICATIONS
#========================================================

R <- 100

#========================================================
# NUMBER OF BOOTSTRAPS
#========================================================

B <- 300

#========================================================
# START LOOP
#========================================================

for(i in 1:R){

  cat("\n====================================\n")

  cat("ZI-ADDITIVE3 : REPLICATION", i, "\n")

  cat("====================================\n")

  #======================================================
  # CREATE TRAIN-TEST SPLIT
  #======================================================

  train_id3 <- sample(

    1:nrow(data1),

    size = floor(
      0.90 * nrow(data1)
    )
  )

  train_3 <- data1[train_id3, ]

  test_3 <- data1[-train_id3, ]

  #======================================================
  # REMOVE MISSING VALUES
  #======================================================

  train_3 <- na.omit(train_3)

  test_3 <- na.omit(test_3)

  #======================================================
  # FIT MODEL
  #======================================================

  zi_add_tr3 <- tryCatch(

    suppressWarnings(

      gam(

        list(

          visits ~

            s(age) +

            s(income) +

            insurance +

            health,

          ~

            s(age) +

            health +

            insurance
        ),

        family = ziplss(),

        data = train_3,

        method = "REML"
      )
    ),

    error = function(e){

      cat(
        "MODEL FAILED\n"
      )

      return(NULL)
    }
  )

  #======================================================
  # SKIP FAILED MODELS
  #======================================================

  if(is.null(zi_add_tr3)){

    next
  }

  #======================================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  hurdle_add_pr3 <- tryCatch(

    predict(

      zi_add_tr3,

      newdata = test_3,

      type = "response"
    ),

    error = function(e){

      rep(

        mean(train_3$visits),

        nrow(test_3)
      )
    }
  )

  #======================================================
  # HANDLE MATRIX PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  if(is.matrix(hurdle_add_pr3)){

    hurdle_add_pr3 <- hurdle_add_pr3[,1]
  }

  #======================================================
  # REMOVE INVALID VALUES
  #======================================================

  keep_test <- complete.cases(

    test_3$visits,

    hurdle_add_pr3

  ) &

    is.finite(hurdle_add_pr3)

  actual_test <- test_3$visits[
    keep_test
  ]

  hurdle_add_pr3 <- hurdle_add_pr3[
    keep_test
  ]

  #======================================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  train_pred_add3 <- predict(

    zi_add_tr3,

    newdata = train_3,

    type = "response"
  )

  #======================================================
  # HANDLE MATRIX PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  if(is.matrix(train_pred_add3)){

    train_pred_add3 <- train_pred_add3[,1]
  }

  #======================================================
  # RESPONSE RESIDUALS
  #======================================================

  resid_add3 <- (

    train_3$visits -

      train_pred_add3
  )

  resid_add3 <- resid_add3[
    is.finite(resid_add3)
  ]

  #======================================================
  # STORAGE MATRIX
  #======================================================

  boot_add3 <- matrix(

    NA,

    nrow = length(actual3),

    ncol = B
  )

  #======================================================
  # RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
  #======================================================

  for(b in 1:B){

    sampled_resid3 <- sample(

      resid_add3,

      size = length(actual3),

      replace = TRUE
    )

    boot_add3[, b] <- (

      hurdle_add_pr3 +

        1.5 * sampled_resid3
    )
  }

  #======================================================
  # NON-NEGATIVE COUNTS
  #======================================================

  boot_add3[
    boot_add3 < 0
  ] <- 0

  #======================================================
  # PREDICTION INTERVALS
  #======================================================

  lower_add3 <- apply(

    boot_add3,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.025,

    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  upper_add3 <- apply(

    boot_add3,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.975,

    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  #======================================================
  # ROUND AFTER QUANTILES
  #======================================================

  lower_add3 <- pmax(

    0,

    round(lower_add3)
  )

  upper_add3 <- pmax(

    0,

    round(upper_add3)
  )

  #======================================================
  # COVERAGE
  #======================================================

  coverage_add3 <- mean(

    actual_test >= lower_add3 &

      actual_test <= upper_add3
  )

  #======================================================
  # WIDTH
  #======================================================

  width_add3 <- mean(

    upper_add3 -

      lower_add3
  )

  #======================================================
  # RMSE
  #======================================================

  #======================================
# ERROR MEASURES : 90:10 SPLIT
#======================================

rmse_add3 <- sqrt(
  mean(
    (actual3 - hurdle_add_pr3)^2,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
)

mae_add3 <- mean(
  abs(actual3 - hurdle_add_pr3),
  na.rm = TRUE
)

me_add3 <- mean(
  actual3 - hurdle_add_pr3,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

mpe_add3 <- mean(
  (actual3 - hurdle_add_pr3) /
    pmax(actual3, 1),
  na.rm = TRUE
) * 100

mape_add3 <- mean(
  abs(
    (actual3 - hurdle_add_pr3) /
      pmax(actual3, 1)
  ),
  na.rm = TRUE
) * 100}



           
```


#GLM COMPARISON
```{r}
#========================================================
# PROFESSIONAL MODEL COMPARISON TABLE
# 70:30 SPLIT
# WITH RANKS + OVERLEAF EXPORT
#========================================================

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

#========================================================
# REMOVE ROW NAMES
#========================================================
#========================================================
# COMBINED COMPARISON TABLE (70:30 SPLIT)
#========================================================

compare_70 <- data.frame(

  Model = c(
    "Generalised Additive Model",
    "Negative Binomial",
    "Generalised Poisson",
    "Poisson",
    "Generalised NB",
    "Quasi-Poisson",
    "COM-Poisson",
    "Discrete Weibull"
  ),

  RMSE = c(
    7.4971,
    8.3637,
    8.4469,
    8.3674,
    8.3644,
    8.3674,
    8.3683,
    8.7352
  ),

  MAE = c(
    5.2360,
    5.6770,
    5.6836,
    5.6880,
    5.6857,
    5.6880,
    5.6936,
    5.7989
  ),

  Coverage = c(
    0.9753,
    0.9707,
    0.9494,
    0.6567,
    0.9670,
    0.9712,
    0.9443,
    0.9746
  
  ),

  Width = c(
    27.2130,
    27.1567,
    23.3114,
    12.0369,
    26.7992,
    29.2431,
    27.7586,
    28.6165
   
  )
)


rownames(compare_70) <- NULL

#========================================================
# RMSE RANK
# SMALLER IS BETTER
#========================================================

compare_70$RMSE_Rank <- rank(
  compare_70$RMSE,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# MAE RANK
# SMALLER IS BETTER
#========================================================

compare_70$MAE_Rank <- rank(
  compare_70$MAE,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# COVERAGE RANK
# LARGER IS BETTER
#========================================================

compare_70$Coverage_Rank <- rank(
  -compare_70$Coverage,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# WIDTH RANK
# SMALLER IS BETTER
#========================================================

compare_70$Width_Rank <- rank(
  compare_70$Width,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# OVERALL SCORE
#========================================================

compare_70$Overall_Score <-
  compare_70$RMSE_Rank +
  compare_70$MAE_Rank +
  compare_70$Coverage_Rank +
  compare_70$Width_Rank

#========================================================
# OVERALL RANK
#========================================================

compare_70$Overall_Rank <- rank(
  compare_70$Overall_Score,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# SORT BY OVERALL RANK
#========================================================

compare_70_final <- compare_70[
  order(compare_70$Overall_Rank),
]

#========================================================
# SELECT COLUMNS
#========================================================

compare_70_final <- compare_70_final[, c(
  "Overall_Rank",
  "Model",
  "RMSE",
  "RMSE_Rank",
  "MAE",
  "MAE_Rank",
  "Coverage",
  "Coverage_Rank",
  "Width",
  "Width_Rank"
)]

#========================================================
# RESET ROW NAMES
#========================================================

rownames(compare_70_final) <- NULL

#========================================================
# VIEW TABLE
#========================================================

compare_70_final

#========================================================
# GENERATE LATEX TABLE
#========================================================

latex_table <- kable(
  compare_70_final,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  align = "c",
  caption = "Performance Comparison of Count Regression Models under the 70:30 Train-Test Split"
)

#========================================================
# SAVE FOR OVERLEAF
#========================================================

writeLines(
  latex_table,
  "compare_70_final.tex"
)

#========================================================
# DISPLAY NICE TABLE IN R
#========================================================

kable(
  compare_70_final,
  caption = "Performance Comparison of Count Regression Models under the 70:30 Train-Test Split",
  align = "c"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    bootstrap_options = c(
      "striped",
      "hover",
      "condensed"
    )
  )

#========================================================
# MESSAGE
#========================================================

cat(
  "\nTable exported successfully as:\n",
  "compare_70_final.tex\n",
  "Upload this file to Overleaf and use:\n",
  "\\input{compare_70_final.tex}\n"
)
getwd()
list.files(pattern = "*.tex")
print(compare_70_final)
```


```{r}

#========================================================
# AIC-BIC COMPARISON TABLE (70:30 SPLIT)
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table70 <- data.frame(

  Model = c(
    "ZIP",
    "ZINB",
    "Hurdle Poisson",
    "Hurdle NB",
    "ZIPO",
    "GPZI",
    "CPZI",
    "ZI Additive",
    "ZILNB"
  ),

  AIC = round(c(
    AIC(zip_1),
    AIC(zinb_1),
    AIC(hurdle_poi1),
    AIC(hurdle_nb1),
    AIC(ZIPO_1),
    AIC(fit1),
    AIC(CPZI_1),
    AIC(zi_add_tr1),
    AIC(hurdle_ZILNB1)
  ), 2),

  BIC = round(c(
    BIC(zip_1),
    BIC(zinb_1),
    BIC(hurdle_poi1),
    BIC(hurdle_nb1),
    BIC(ZIPO_1),
    BIC(fit1),
    BIC(CPZI_1),
    BIC(zi_add_tr1),
    BIC(hurdle_ZILNB1)
  ), 2)
)

#========================================================
# RANKS
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table70$AIC_Rank <- rank(
  AIC_BIC_Table70$AIC,
  ties.method = "min"
)

AIC_BIC_Table70$BIC_Rank <- rank(
  AIC_BIC_Table70$BIC,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# OVERALL SCORE
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table70$Overall_Score <-
  AIC_BIC_Table70$AIC_Rank +
  AIC_BIC_Table70$BIC_Rank

AIC_BIC_Table70$Overall_Rank <- rank(
  AIC_BIC_Table70$Overall_Score,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# SORT BY OVERALL RANK
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table70 <- AIC_BIC_Table70[
  order(AIC_BIC_Table70$Overall_Rank),
]

rownames(AIC_BIC_Table70) <- NULL

#========================================================
# FINAL TABLE
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table70 <- AIC_BIC_Table70[, c(
  "Overall_Rank",
  "Model",
  "AIC",
  "AIC_Rank",
  "BIC",
  "BIC_Rank"
)]

AIC_BIC_Table70

#========================================================
# PROFESSIONAL DISPLAY
#========================================================

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

kable(
  AIC_BIC_Table70,
  caption = "Model Selection Criteria Based on AIC and BIC (70:30 Split)",
  align = "c"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    bootstrap_options = c(
      "striped",
      "hover",
      "condensed"
    )
  )

#========================================================
# EXPORT TO OVERLEAF
#========================================================

latex_aic70 <- kable(
  AIC_BIC_Table70,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  align = "c",
  caption = "Model Selection Criteria Based on AIC and BIC (70:30 Split)"
)

writeLines(
  latex_aic70,
  "AIC_BIC_Table70.tex"
)
```

#80
```{r}

#========================================================
# PROFESSIONAL MODEL COMPARISON TABLE
# 80:20 SPLIT
#========================================================
compare_80 <- data.frame(

  Model = c(
    "Generalised NB",
    "Generalised Additive Model",
    "Negative Binomial",
    "Poisson",
    "Quasi-Poisson",
    "COM-Poisson",
    "Generalised Poisson",
    "Discrete Weibull"
  ),

  RMSE = c(
    8.4576,
    8.4587,
    8.4638,
    8.4686,
    8.4686,
    8.5012,
    8.5371,
    8.8400
   
  ),

  MAE = c(
    5.7208,
    5.7247,
    5.7189,
    5.7307,
    5.7307,
    5.7405,
    5.7141,
    5.8337
  ),

  Coverage = c(
    0.9669,
    0.9673,
    0.9703,
    0.6542,
    0.9710,
    0.9511,
    0.9503,
    0.9743
  ),

  Width = c(
    26.7628,
    27.1386,
    27.1720,
    12.0485,
    29.2683,
    28.2676,
    23.0464,
    28.6695
  )
)

rownames(compare_80) <- NULL
#========================================================
# COMBINED COMPARISON TABLE (80:20 SPLIT)
#========================================================


#========================================================
# RANKS
#========================================================

compare_80$RMSE_Rank <- rank(
  compare_80$RMSE,
  ties.method = "min"
)

compare_80$MAE_Rank <- rank(
  compare_80$MAE,
  ties.method = "min"
)

compare_80$Coverage_Rank <- rank(
  -compare_80$Coverage,
  ties.method = "min"
)

compare_80$Width_Rank <- rank(
  compare_80$Width,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# OVERALL SCORE
#========================================================

compare_80$Overall_Score <-
  compare_80$RMSE_Rank +
  compare_80$MAE_Rank +
  compare_80$Coverage_Rank +
  compare_80$Width_Rank

compare_80$Overall_Rank <- rank(
  compare_80$Overall_Score,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# SORT BY OVERALL RANK
#========================================================

compare_80_final <- compare_80[
  order(compare_80$Overall_Rank),
]

#========================================================
# FINAL TABLE
#========================================================

compare_80_final <- compare_80_final[, c(
  "Overall_Rank",
  "Model",
  "RMSE",
  "RMSE_Rank",
  "MAE",
  "MAE_Rank",
  "Coverage",
  "Coverage_Rank",
  "Width",
  "Width_Rank"
)]

rownames(compare_80_final) <- NULL

compare_80_final

#========================================================
# DISPLAY TABLE
#========================================================

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

kable(
  compare_80_final,
  caption = "Performance Comparison of Count Regression Models under the 80:20 Train--Test Split",
  align = "c"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    bootstrap_options = c(
      "striped",
      "hover",
      "condensed"
    )
  )

#========================================================
# EXPORT TO OVERLEAF
#========================================================

latex_table80 <- kable(
  compare_80_final,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  align = "c",
  caption = "Performance Comparison of Count Regression Models under the 80:20 Train--Test Split"
)

writeLines(
  latex_table80,
  "compare_80_final.tex"
)

#========================================================
# AIC-BIC COMPARISON TABLE (80:20 SPLIT)
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table80 <- data.frame(

  Model = c(
    "ZIP",
    "ZINB",
    "Hurdle Poisson",
    "Hurdle NB",
    "ZIPO",
    "GPZI",
    "CPZI",
    "ZI Additive",
    "ZILNB"
  ),

  AIC = round(c(
    AIC(zip_2),
    AIC(zinb_2),
    AIC(hurdle_poi2),
    AIC(hurdle_nb2),
    AIC(ZIPO_2),
    AIC(fit2),
    AIC(CPZI_2),
    AIC(zi_add_tr2),
    AIC(hurdle_ZILNB2)
  ), 2),

  BIC = round(c(
    BIC(zip_2),
    BIC(zinb_2),
    BIC(hurdle_poi2),
    BIC(hurdle_nb2),
    BIC(ZIPO_2),
    BIC(fit2),
    BIC(CPZI_2),
    BIC(zi_add_tr2),
    BIC(hurdle_ZILNB2)
  ), 2)
)

#========================================================
# RANKS
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table80$AIC_Rank <- rank(
  AIC_BIC_Table80$AIC,
  ties.method = "min"
)

AIC_BIC_Table80$BIC_Rank <- rank(
  AIC_BIC_Table80$BIC,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# OVERALL SCORE
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table80$Overall_Score <-
  AIC_BIC_Table80$AIC_Rank +
  AIC_BIC_Table80$BIC_Rank

AIC_BIC_Table80$Overall_Rank <- rank(
  AIC_BIC_Table80$Overall_Score,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# SORT BY OVERALL RANK
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table80 <- AIC_BIC_Table80[
  order(AIC_BIC_Table80$Overall_Rank),
]

rownames(AIC_BIC_Table80) <- NULL

#========================================================
# FINAL TABLE
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table80 <- AIC_BIC_Table80[, c(
  "Overall_Rank",
  "Model",
  "AIC",
  "AIC_Rank",
  "BIC",
  "BIC_Rank"
)]

AIC_BIC_Table80

#========================================================
# DISPLAY TABLE
#========================================================

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

kable(
  AIC_BIC_Table80,
  caption = "Model Selection Criteria Based on AIC and BIC (80:20 Split)",
  align = "c"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    bootstrap_options = c(
      "striped",
      "hover",
      "condensed"
    )
  )

#========================================================
# EXPORT TO OVERLEAF
#========================================================

latex_aic80 <- kable(
  AIC_BIC_Table80,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  align = "c",
  caption = "Model Selection Criteria Based on AIC and BIC (80:20 Split)"
)

writeLines(
  latex_aic80,
  "AIC_BIC_Table80.tex"
)
print(compare_80_final)
```

#90
```{r}

#========================================================
# AIC-BIC COMPARISON TABLE (90:10 SPLIT)
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table90 <- data.frame(

  Model = c(
    "ZIP",
    "ZINB",
    "Hurdle Poisson",
    "Hurdle NB",
    "ZIPO",
    "GPZI",
    "CPZI",
    "ZI Additive",
    "ZILNB"
  ),

  AIC = round(c(
    AIC(zip_3),
    AIC(zinb_3),
    AIC(hurdle_poi3),
    AIC(hurdle_nb3),
    AIC(ZIPO_3),
    AIC(fit3),
    AIC(CPZI_3),
    AIC(zi_add_tr3),
    AIC(hurdle_ZILNB3)
  ), 2),

  BIC = round(c(
    BIC(zip_3),
    BIC(zinb_3),
    BIC(hurdle_poi3),
    BIC(hurdle_nb3),
    BIC(ZIPO_3),
    BIC(fit3),
    BIC(CPZI_3),
    BIC(zi_add_tr3),
    BIC(hurdle_ZILNB3)
  ), 2)
)

#========================================================
# AIC RANK
# SMALLER IS BETTER
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table90$AIC_Rank <- rank(
  AIC_BIC_Table90$AIC,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# BIC RANK
# SMALLER IS BETTER
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table90$BIC_Rank <- rank(
  AIC_BIC_Table90$BIC,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# OVERALL SCORE
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table90$Overall_Score <-
  AIC_BIC_Table90$AIC_Rank +
  AIC_BIC_Table90$BIC_Rank

#========================================================
# OVERALL RANK
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table90$Overall_Rank <- rank(
  AIC_BIC_Table90$Overall_Score,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# SORT BY OVERALL RANK
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table90 <- AIC_BIC_Table90[
  order(AIC_BIC_Table90$Overall_Rank),
]

rownames(AIC_BIC_Table90) <- NULL

#========================================================
# FINAL TABLE
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table90 <- AIC_BIC_Table90[, c(
  "Overall_Rank",
  "Model",
  "AIC",
  "AIC_Rank",
  "BIC",
  "BIC_Rank"
)]

#========================================================
# VIEW TABLE
#========================================================

AIC_BIC_Table90

#========================================================
# PROFESSIONAL DISPLAY
#========================================================

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

kable(
  AIC_BIC_Table90,
  caption = "Model Selection Criteria Based on AIC and BIC (90:10 Split)",
  align = "c"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    bootstrap_options = c(
      "striped",
      "hover",
      "condensed"
    )
  )

#========================================================
# EXPORT TO OVERLEAF
#========================================================

latex_aic90 <- kable(
  AIC_BIC_Table90,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  align = "c",
  caption = "Model Selection Criteria Based on AIC and BIC (90:10 Split)"
)

writeLines(
  latex_aic90,
  "AIC_BIC_Table90.tex"
)

#========================================================
# MESSAGE
#========================================================

cat(
  "\nTable exported successfully as:\n",
  "AIC_BIC_Table90.tex\n",
  "Upload this file to Overleaf and use:\n",
  "\\input{AIC_BIC_Table90.tex}\n"
)

#========================================================
# CHECK FILE CREATED
#========================================================

getwd()
list.files(pattern = "*.tex")

#========================================================
# PROFESSIONAL MODEL COMPARISON TABLE
# 90:10 SPLIT
# WITH RANKS + OVERLEAF EXPORT
#========================================================

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

#========================================================
# REMOVE ROW NAMES
#========================================================
#========================================================
# COUNT REGRESSION MODELS ONLY (90:10 SPLIT)
#========================================================

compare_90 <- data.frame(

  Model = c(
    "Negative Binomial",
    "Generalised NB",
    "Generalised Additive Model",
    "Poisson",
    "Generalised Poisson",
    "Quasi-Poisson",
    "COM-Poisson",
    "Discrete Weibull"
  ),

  RMSE = c(
    8.0877,
    8.0868,
    8.0887,
    8.0933,
    8.1462,
    8.0933,
    8.1178,
    8.4274
  ),

  MAE = c(
    5.6349,
    5.6341,
    5.6374,
    5.6443,
    5.6409,
    5.6443,
    5.6546,
    5.7841
  ),

  Coverage = c(
    0.9728,
    0.9685,
    0.9693,
    0.6534,
    0.9470,
    0.9726,
    0.9531,
    0.9755
  ),

  Width = c(
    27.1720,
    26.7628,
    27.1386,
    12.0485,
    23.0464,
    29.2683,
    28.2676,
    28.6695
  )
)

rownames(compare_90) <- NULL
rownames(compare_90) <- NULL

#========================================================
# RMSE RANK
# SMALLER IS BETTER
#========================================================

compare_90$RMSE_Rank <- rank(
  compare_90$RMSE,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# MAE RANK
# SMALLER IS BETTER
#========================================================

compare_90$MAE_Rank <- rank(
  compare_90$MAE,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# COVERAGE RANK
# LARGER IS BETTER
#========================================================

compare_90$Coverage_Rank <- rank(
  -compare_90$Coverage,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# WIDTH RANK
# SMALLER IS BETTER
#========================================================

compare_90$Width_Rank <- rank(
  compare_90$Width,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# OVERALL SCORE
#========================================================

compare_90$Overall_Score <-
  compare_90$RMSE_Rank +
  compare_90$MAE_Rank +
  compare_90$Coverage_Rank +
  compare_90$Width_Rank

#========================================================
# OVERALL RANK
#========================================================

compare_90$Overall_Rank <- rank(
  compare_90$Overall_Score,
  ties.method = "min"
)

#========================================================
# SORT BY OVERALL RANK
#========================================================

compare_90_final <- compare_90[
  order(compare_90$Overall_Rank),
]

#========================================================
# SELECT COLUMNS
#========================================================

compare_90_final <- compare_90_final[, c(
  "Overall_Rank",
  "Model",
  "RMSE",
  "RMSE_Rank",
  "MAE",
  "MAE_Rank",
  "Coverage",
  "Coverage_Rank",
  "Width",
  "Width_Rank"
)]

#========================================================
# RESET ROW NAMES
#========================================================

rownames(compare_90_final) <- NULL

#========================================================
# VIEW TABLE
#========================================================

compare_90_final

#========================================================
# DISPLAY NICE TABLE IN R
#========================================================

kable(
  compare_90_final,
  caption = "Performance Comparison of Count Regression Models under the 90:10 Train-Test Split",
  align = "c"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    bootstrap_options = c(
      "striped",
      "hover",
      "condensed"
    )
  )

#========================================================
# EXPORT TO OVERLEAF
#========================================================

latex_compare90 <- kable(
  compare_90_final,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  align = "c",
  caption = "Performance Comparison of Count Regression Models under the 90:10 Train-Test Split",
  label = "tab:compare90"
)

writeLines(
  latex_compare90,
  "compare_90_final.tex"
)

#========================================================
# MESSAGE
#========================================================

cat(
  "\nTable exported successfully as:\n",
  "compare_90_final.tex\n",
  "Upload this file to Overleaf and use:\n",
  "\\input{compare_90_final.tex}\n"
)

print(compare_90_final)
```




##MACHINE LEARNING

#Random-Forest

```{r}
#========================================================
# RANDOM FOREST
# 70:30 SPLIT
# 100 REPLICATIONS
# CONFORMAL PREDICTION INTERVAL
#========================================================

#========================================================
# LOAD PACKAGES
#========================================================

library(randomForest)
library(forecast)

#========================================================
# STORAGE
#========================================================

results_rf1 <- data.frame()

#========================================================
# REPRODUCIBILITY
#========================================================

set.seed(123)

#========================================================
# NUMBER OF REPLICATIONS
#========================================================

R <- 100

#========================================================
# LOOP
#========================================================

for(i in 1:R){

  cat("\n====================================\n")
  cat("RF1 : REPLICATION", i, "\n")
  cat("====================================\n")

  #======================================================
  # 70:30 SPLIT
  #======================================================

  train_id1 <- sample(
    1:nrow(data1),
    size = floor(0.70 * nrow(data1))
  )

  train_1 <- data1[train_id1, ]
  test_1  <- data1[-train_id1, ]

  #======================================================
  # REMOVE MISSING VALUES
  #======================================================

  train_1 <- na.omit(train_1)
  test_1  <- na.omit(test_1)

  #======================================================
  # FIT RANDOM FOREST
  #======================================================

  rf_model1 <- randomForest(

    visits ~

      hospital +

      chronic +

      health +

      adl +

      insurance +

      income +

      age,

    data = train_1,

    ntree = 300,

    importance = TRUE
  )

  #======================================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  pred_rf1 <- predict(
    rf_model1,
    newdata = test_1
  )

  pred_rf1 <- round(pred_rf1)

  #======================================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  train_pred_rf1 <- predict(
    rf_model1,
    newdata = train_1
  )

  #======================================================
  # ACTUAL VALUES
  #======================================================

  actual1 <- test_1$visits

  #======================================================
  # ACCURACY MEASURES
  #======================================================

  acc_rf1 <- as.data.frame(
    forecast::accuracy(
      pred_rf1,
      actual1
    )
  )

  #======================================================
  # CONFORMAL RESIDUALS
  #======================================================

  resid_rf1 <- abs(
    train_1$visits -
      train_pred_rf1
  )

  resid_rf1 <- resid_rf1[
    is.finite(resid_rf1)
  ]

  #======================================================
  # 95% CONFORMAL QUANTILE
  #======================================================

  qhat_rf1 <- quantile(
    resid_rf1,
    probs = 0.95,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  #======================================================
  # PREDICTION INTERVALS
  #======================================================

  lower_rf1 <- pred_rf1 - qhat_rf1

  upper_rf1 <- pred_rf1 + qhat_rf1

  #======================================================
  # NON-NEGATIVE COUNTS
  #======================================================

  lower_rf1 <- pmax(
    0,
    round(lower_rf1)
  )

  upper_rf1 <- round(
    upper_rf1
  )

  #======================================================
  # COVERAGE
  #======================================================

  coverage_rf1 <- mean(
    actual1 >= lower_rf1 &
      actual1 <= upper_rf1
  )

  #======================================================
  # WIDTH
  #======================================================

  width_rf1 <- mean(
    upper_rf1 -
      lower_rf1
  )

  #======================================================
  # STORE SPLIT RESULTS
  #======================================================

  split_rf1 <- data.frame(
    Actual = actual1,
    Predicted = pred_rf1,
    Lower_PI = lower_rf1,
    Upper_PI = upper_rf1
  )

  assign(
    paste0(
      "RF1_SPLIT_",
      i
    ),
    split_rf1
  )

  #======================================================
  # STORE RESULTS
  #======================================================

  results_rf1 <- rbind(

    results_rf1,

    data.frame(

      Replication = i,

      Coverage = coverage_rf1,

      Width = width_rf1,

      ME = acc_rf1[
        "Test set",
        "ME"
      ],

      RMSE = acc_rf1[
        "Test set",
        "RMSE"
      ],

      MAE = acc_rf1[
        "Test set",
        "MAE"
      ],

      MAPE = acc_rf1[
        "Test set",
        "MAPE"
      ]
    )
  )

  #======================================================
  # DISPLAY
  #======================================================

  cat(
    "Coverage =",
    coverage_rf1,
    "\n"
  )

  cat(
    "Width =",
    width_rf1,
    "\n"
  )
}

#========================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
#========================================================

summary_rf1 <- data.frame(

  Mean_Coverage =
    mean(
      results_rf1$Coverage,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),

  Mean_Width =
    mean(
      results_rf1$Width,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),

  Mean_ME =
    mean(
      results_rf1$ME,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),

  Mean_RMSE =
    mean(
      results_rf1$RMSE,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),

  Mean_MAE =
    mean(
      results_rf1$MAE,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),

  Mean_MAPE =
    mean(
      results_rf1$MAPE,
      na.rm = TRUE
    )
)

summary_rf1


```
#80
```{r}
#========================================================
# RANDOM FOREST
# 80:20 SPLIT
# 100 REPLICATIONS
# CONFORMAL PREDICTION INTERVAL
#========================================================

#========================================================
# STORAGE
#========================================================

results_rf2 <- data.frame()

#========================================================
# REPRODUCIBILITY
#========================================================

set.seed(123)

R <- 100

#========================================================
# LOOP
#========================================================

for(i in 1:R){

  cat("\n====================================\n")
  cat("RF2 : REPLICATION", i, "\n")
  cat("====================================\n")

  #======================================================
  # 80:20 SPLIT
  #======================================================

  train_id2 <- sample(
    1:nrow(data1),
    size = floor(0.80*nrow(data1))
  )

  train_2 <- data1[train_id2, ]
  test_2  <- data1[-train_id2, ]

  train_2 <- na.omit(train_2)
  test_2  <- na.omit(test_2)

  #======================================================
  # FIT RF
  #======================================================

  rf_model2 <- randomForest(

    visits ~
      hospital +
      chronic +
      health +
      adl +
      insurance +
      income +
      age,

    data = train_2,

    ntree = 300,

    importance = TRUE
  )

  #======================================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  pred_rf2 <- predict(
    rf_model2,
    newdata = test_2
  )

  pred_rf2 <- round(pred_rf2)

  train_pred_rf2 <- predict(
    rf_model2,
    newdata = train_2
  )

  actual2 <- test_2$visits

  #======================================================
  # ACCURACY
  #======================================================

  acc_rf2 <- as.data.frame(
    forecast::accuracy(
      pred_rf2,
      actual2
    )
  )

  #======================================================
  # CONFORMAL PI
  #======================================================

  resid_rf2 <- abs(
    train_2$visits -
      train_pred_rf2
  )

  qhat_rf2 <- quantile(
    resid_rf2,
    probs = 0.95,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  lower_rf2 <- pmax(
    0,
    round(pred_rf2 - qhat_rf2)
  )

  upper_rf2 <- round(
    pred_rf2 + qhat_rf2
  )

  #======================================================
  # COVERAGE & WIDTH
  #======================================================

  coverage_rf2 <- mean(
    actual2 >= lower_rf2 &
      actual2 <= upper_rf2
  )

  width_rf2 <- mean(
    upper_rf2 - lower_rf2
  )

  #======================================================
  # STORE RESULTS
  #======================================================

  results_rf2 <- rbind(
    results_rf2,
    data.frame(
      Replication = i,
      Coverage = coverage_rf2,
      Width = width_rf2,
      ME = acc_rf2["Test set","ME"],
      RMSE = acc_rf2["Test set","RMSE"],
      MAE = acc_rf2["Test set","MAE"],
      MAPE = acc_rf2["Test set","MAPE"]
    )
  )
}

#========================================================
# SUMMARY
#========================================================

summary_rf2 <- data.frame(

  Mean_Coverage = mean(results_rf2$Coverage),

  Mean_Width = mean(results_rf2$Width),

  Mean_ME = mean(results_rf2$ME),

  Mean_RMSE = mean(results_rf2$RMSE),

  Mean_MAE = mean(results_rf2$MAE),

  Mean_MAPE = mean(results_rf2$MAPE)
)

summary_rf2
 
```

#90
```{r}
#========================================================
# RANDOM FOREST
# 90:10 SPLIT
# 100 REPLICATIONS
# CONFORMAL PREDICTION INTERVAL
#========================================================

#========================================================
# LOAD PACKAGES
#========================================================

library(randomForest)
library(forecast)

#========================================================
# STORAGE
#========================================================

results_rf3 <- data.frame()

#========================================================
# REPRODUCIBILITY
#========================================================

set.seed(123)

R <- 100

#========================================================
# LOOP
#========================================================

for(i in 1:R){

  cat("\n====================================\n")
  cat("RF3 : REPLICATION", i, "\n")
  cat("====================================\n")

  #======================================================
  # 90:10 SPLIT
  #======================================================

  train_id3 <- sample(
    1:nrow(data1),
    size = floor(0.90*nrow(data1))
  )

  train_3 <- data1[train_id3, ]
  test_3  <- data1[-train_id3, ]

  train_3 <- na.omit(train_3)
  test_3  <- na.omit(test_3)

  #======================================================
  # FIT RF
  #======================================================

  rf_model3 <- randomForest(

    visits ~
      hospital +
      chronic +
      health +
      adl +
      insurance +
      income +
      age,

    data = train_3,

    ntree = 300,

    importance = TRUE
  )

  #======================================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #======================================================

  pred_rf3 <- predict(
    rf_model3,
    newdata = test_3
  )

  pred_rf3 <- round(pred_rf3)

  train_pred_rf3 <- predict(
    rf_model3,
    newdata = train_3
  )

  actual3 <- test_3$visits

  #======================================================
  # ACCURACY
  #======================================================

  acc_rf3 <- as.data.frame(
    forecast::accuracy(
      pred_rf3,
      actual3
    )
  )

  #======================================================
  # CONFORMAL PI
  #======================================================

  resid_rf3 <- abs(
    train_3$visits -
      train_pred_rf3
  )

  qhat_rf3 <- quantile(
    resid_rf3,
    probs = 0.95,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

  lower_rf3 <- pmax(
    0,
    round(pred_rf3 - qhat_rf3)
  )

  upper_rf3 <- round(
    pred_rf3 + qhat_rf3
  )

  #======================================================
  # COVERAGE & WIDTH
  #======================================================

  coverage_rf3 <- mean(
    actual3 >= lower_rf3 &
      actual3 <= upper_rf3
```
